title: pericallis papyracea subsp. hillebrandii
intro: Pericallis papyracea subsp. hillebrandii, also known as the Hawaiian silversword, is a rare and endangered succulent plant native to the island of Hawaii. This plant is believed to have medicinal properties, with roots that contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds and infections. The health benefits of Pericallis papyracea subsp. hillebrandii include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the roots in water or alcohol to create teas, tinctures, or salves that can be applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
